phase_name: post-milestone — real-repo benchmark of sourcecode + product strategy
session_type: field benchmark on a real maintained OSS repo + product/monetization strategy

completed_tasks.name: Benchmark PR #3131 — Fix #3124 open-redirect (protocol-relative bypass) in LocalRedirectStrategy
completed_tasks.detail: BroadleafCommerce (real, maintained). Reject protocol-relative '//host' + backslash variants before the startsWith('/') gate. Verified 6/6 JUnit via standalone javac + .m2 jars + JUnitCore (no mvn in env). PR open from fork HarounDominique. SHOWCASE WEAK: impact/impact-chain returned 0 callers (DI/interface-mediated blindspot), so the tool did NOT light up here. Competing open PR #3126 cited honestly.

completed_tasks.name: Benchmark PR #3132 — Fix #2280 add index on BLC_FIELD.ABBREVIATION
completed_tasks.detail: SHOWCASE STRONG. impact-chain lit up (readFieldByAbbreviation: 4 direct / 13 transitive callers, cacheable hot path, <100ms over 7488-file repo). Tool guided issue selection AND ruled out #2232 as already-fixed in 7.0.x (true negative). One-line @Index addition matching ENTITY_TYPE_INDEX convention. PR open from fork.

completed_tasks.name: Benchmark report + honest scoring
completed_tasks.detail: atlas-cli/.planning/benchmark-broadleaf-3124.md. Scored: performance 8.5-9/10, value-to-issue 3.5/10 (#3124) vs 8/10 (#2280). Verdict: tool brilla on direct call-chain blast-radius / migration, weak on DI-mediated security.

completed_tasks.name: Product/monetization strategy documented
completed_tasks.detail: atlas-cli/.planning/ESTRATEGIA-PRODUCTO-TODO.md + memory project_product_strategy. Focus deterministic Java/Spring intel; NO security-detection scope (enrichment only); fix DI/interface call-graph blindspot as core quality; monetize open-core + per-seat Pro + per-project migration SKU; replace class-count cap with command-tier+seat gating; watch OpenRewrite + IntelliJ.

remaining_tasks.name: Watch PR #3131 + #3132 review feedback from Broadleaf maintainers
remaining_tasks.needs: maintainer response; #3131 contends with competing PR #3126

remaining_tasks.name: CORE: fix DI/interface-mediated call-graph blindspot
remaining_tasks.needs: reproduce with LocalRedirectStrategy; impact-chain should report the login flows that wire it. High leverage — see strategy doc section 3

remaining_tasks.name: Revisit prior post-milestone open items audit #4/#6 (custom-annotation security) THROUGH new strategy lens
remaining_tasks.needs: decision: per new strategy, security-detection scope is deprioritized -> reframe as enrichment, not a detector

human_actions_pending.action: Optionally indicate in PR bodies that analysis used sourcecode
human_actions_pending.context: User decided to leave PR bodies generic (self-promo risk). No action unless reversed.

decisions.decision: Focus product on deterministic structural intel; do NOT build security detection
decisions.rationale: Benchmark proved tool is decisive on direct call-chain blast-radius (#2280) but useless on DI-mediated security (#3124); security is a red ocean (Snyk/Semgrep/CodeQL)

decisions.decision: Monetization: open-core + PLG via MCP, per-seat Pro on high-value verbs, per-project migration SKU; replace class-count cap with command-tier+seat gating
decisions.rationale: Class-count cap blocks the aha on the best prospect (large monolith) on first run; meters input not value; never cap onboarding

decisions.decision: Picked #2280 over #2232/#3124-style for the strong-showcase PR
decisions.rationale: #2232 already fixed in 7.0.x; #2280 hot-path with rich blast radius + safe verifiable fix

decisions.decision: Leave PR bodies generic (no sourcecode mention)
decisions.rationale: Maintainers may read vendor-tool mention as self-promo

git_state: atlas-cli clean, HEAD 211d697 (.planning gitignored except .continue-here.md + HANDOFF.json). broadleaf-fieldtest: 2 branches pushed to fork HarounDominique, 2 PRs OPEN (#3131, #3132); local HEAD on fix/2280-index-blc-field-abbreviation.
next_action: Either (a) monitor PR #3131/#3132 review, (b) start the CORE DI/interface call-graph blindspot fix (highest-leverage, on-strategy), or (c) reframe the deferred security audit items #4/#6 as enrichment per new strategy. No active blockers.
context_notes: This was a benchmark+strategy session, NOT phase execution. Two real PRs opened on a maintained OSS repo (Broadleaf) to prove sourcecode value honestly. Key insight: the tool's edge is real but narrow (direct call-chain impact + migration); honest-benchmarks rule held throughout (reported what did NOT work). Verification of Java fixes done standalone (javac + ~/.m2 jars + JUnitCore) because no mvn/mvnw in env. gh CLI authenticated as HarounDominique. Strategy fully captured in ESTRATEGIA-PRODUCTO-TODO.md.
